---
title: "Teaching"
subtitle: "Finance education grounded in economic intuition, analytical discipline, and practical decision-making."
page-layout: article
toc: false
---

::: {.teaching-intro}
I teach corporate finance and capital budgeting at the undergraduate level at the School of Economics and Management, University of Porto.
:::

::: {.course-grid}
::: {.course-card}
<span class="course-level">Bachelor in Management</span>

## Capital Budgeting

How firms evaluate investment opportunities when cash flows unfold over time and outcomes are uncertain.

<div class="course-topics">
<span>Investment criteria</span>
<span>Cash-flow estimation</span>
<span>Cost of capital</span>
<span>Risk and flexibility</span>
</div>
:::

::: {.course-card}
<span class="course-level">Bachelor in Economics</span>

## Corporate Finance

The fundamental choices that determine how firms invest, finance operations, and distribute value.

<div class="course-topics">
<span>Valuation</span>
<span>Capital structure</span>
<span>Payout policy</span>
<span>Corporate decisions</span>
</div>
:::
:::

::: {.teaching-note}
## For students

Course materials, assessment information, and announcements are distributed through the University of Porto's institutional learning systems.
:::
